labeled_by: claude (cross-referenced against checked-in audit doc at projects/ultra-gal-origin/audits/pages-01-07-audit-2026-05-16.md)
label_source_note: Ground-truth labels were derived from the human-validated QA audit document committed into the repo for pages 01-07 of ultra-gal-origin (the only project in this checkout with accepted panels + a per-panel audit). The audit doc was authored by a Comic-production QA subagent and reviewed by Matt before commit. Future iterations should incorporate independent labels from Matt and Magnamus directly (per Discord notes) to remove the LLM-in-the-loop bias.

defect_taxonomy.composite_mismatch: foreground/background lighting, shadows, or scale don't match (looks copy-pasted)
defect_taxonomy.hair_discontinuity: character's hair color, length, or style jumped vs canonical or vs adjacent panel
defect_taxonomy.costume_discontinuity: costume color, shape, or specific garment changed unexpectedly (e.g. green→navy crewneck, missing overalls, missing lab coat)
defect_taxonomy.scale_error: background extras or props at wrong scale relative to foreground subject
defect_taxonomy.empty_speech_bubble: speech bubble exists but tail points to wrong character, or bubble is empty
defect_taxonomy.tier_visualization_mismatch: transformation tier declared in shotlist (e.g. tier 7) but rendered closer to a lower tier (3-4)
defect_taxonomy.prompt_bloat_artifact: visible evidence the prompt had repeated/contradictory directives (e.g. baked-in 2D illustration drift)
defect_taxonomy.lettering_error: added category — typos in dialogue, duplicate bubbles, repeated identical lines (observed in audit doc)
defect_taxonomy.character_count_error: added category — declared cast count doesn't match panel (e.g. character missing from frame entirely)
defect_taxonomy.character_identity_swap: added category — wrong character occupies a scripted role (e.g. Lenny replaced by Carl)

added_categories_rationale: lettering_error, character_count_error, and character_identity_swap were added because the audit doc surfaces them repeatedly and they're real defects Matt has flagged in production. Sticking strictly to the task's 7 categories would force these into ill-fitting buckets and weaken the experiment's measurement.

coverage_gaps: composite_mismatch: 0 examples in this set — cannot measure recall for this HIGH-priority category in v1. Future labeled sets should hand-pick panels exhibiting foreground/background light mismatch.
scale_error: 0 examples — same caveat (HIGH priority, can't measure).
tier_visualization_mismatch: 0 examples — would need to source from a chun-li-iron-discipline or emma-frost-ascension run with declared-vs-rendered tier mismatches.
prompt_bloat_artifact: 0 examples — would need a chun-li-test run with documented L7 Case B 2D drift.

methodology_notes: The label source is a single human-validated audit doc; the same project was used for all 20 panels. This limits generalization. The recommendation will flag this as the primary thing to fix before shipping the audit pipeline-wide.

Per the spawn task: this is an experiment with an explicit stop condition (5 iterations or recall>=80% on HIGH categories). The narrow source means the v1 measurement only seriously stresses costume_discontinuity and hair_discontinuity.

The audit doc references canonical wardrobe/hair specs (e.g. Heather=GREEN crewneck, auburn-red hair). The vision audit will be given the cast description from shotlist.json so its judgements aren't unmoored from canonical.
